Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.

When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 165 actions; a strong score needs about 39 or fewer. You are 4.2x over the target.
NET-ZERO WASTE: your last 68 actions returned the board to a state already seen 68 actions ago — that round-trip made no progress yet still cost 68 actions. Do not extend-and-retract or oscillate; a sequence that ends where it began is pure waste.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

- Blue rectangle: rows 16-19, cols 24-43 (moved RIGHT 3 steps from cols 12-31)
- Blocks: still at rows 1-7, cols 36-39 (didn't fall)

The blocks didn't fall even though the blue rectangle is now below them (cols 24-43 overlaps with cols 36-39). So the blocks don't fall just because the blue rectangle is below them.
Let me think about this differently. What if the blocks fall when I press DOWN? Like, pressing DOWN makes the blue rectangle move down AND the blocks fall?
But I already tried pressing DOWN before, and the blue rectangle moved down but the blocks didn't fall.
Wait, let me re-examine. When I pressed DOWN earlier, the blue rectangle moved from rows 12-15 to rows 16-19. But the blocks stayed at rows 1-7. So pressing DOWN doesn't make the blocks fall.
Hmm, what if the blocks fall when the blue rectangle is at a specific position AND I press a specific key?
Or what if the blocks fall when the timer bar reaches a certain position?
Actually, let me try a completely different approach. What if the goal is to click on the blocks to make them fall? But I already tried clicking on the blocks, and nothing happened.
Wait, let me try clicking on the blocks again, but this time with the blue rectangle positioned below them. Maybe the blocks fall when I click on them AND the blue rectangle is below them.
Actually, I've been spending too many actions on this level. Let me try a different approach: what if the goal is simply to move the blue rectangle to a specific position and then press SPACE?
Let me try moving the blue rectangle to the right container's position (cols 40-51) and then pressing SPACE.
The blue rectangle is at cols 24-43. The right container is at cols 40-51. To align with the right container, I need to move the blue rectangle RIGHT by 1 step (4 cols), which would put it at cols 28-47. This still doesn't fully align with cols 40-51.
If I move RIGHT by 2 steps (8 cols), the blue rectangle would be at cols 32-51. This would align with the right container's right edge (col 51).
Let me try moving RIGHT 2 steps and then pressing SPACE.
But wait, I've already tried pressing SPACE, and it just restarted the game. So pressing SPACE probably doesn't complete the level.
Hmm, let me think about this more carefully. What actions have I tried?
1. Arrow keys (UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT) - move the blue rectangle
2. SPACE - restarts the game
3. MOUSE on timer bar - extends the green section
4. MOUSE on blocks - nothing
5. MOUSE on blue rectangle - nothing
6. MOUSE on containers - nothing
7. MOUSE on background - nothing
8. MOUSE on gray bar - nothing
What haven't I tried?
- MOUSE on the white section of the timer bar (the right part)
- MOUSE on the boundary between the green and white sections of the timer bar
- MOUSE on the boundary between the blocks and the background
- MOUSE on the boundary between the blue rectangle and the background
- MOUSE on the boundary between the containers and the background
- MOUSE on the boundary between the gray bar and the background
Let me try clicking on the white section of the timer bar (the right part, cols 55-63).
Actually wait, I just noticed something. The timer bar has a green section (N) and a white section (W). When I click on the green section, the green section extends to the click position. But what if clicking on the white section does something different?
Let me try clicking on the white section of the timer bar.
